commands_run.notes: Ran 3 targeted reviewdog exporter tests; all passed.
Ran the full repository regression suite; 36 tests passed.
Generated a real health report under the permitted scratch directory for smoke validation.
Exported 93 file-backed reviewdog diagnostics from a real Repo Manager smell report.

commands_run.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 - <<'PY' ... validate workflow YAML, action pins, and fork PR guard ... PY
commands_run.notes: Parsed reviewer.yml and verified both jobs, immutable action pins, absence of pull_request_target, absence of local call_agent usage, and the fork PR guard.

Scorer reported total=98: correctness 40/40, hygiene 18/20, scope control 15/15, handoff 15/15, and board consistency 10/10. The only deduction is for legitimate new implementation files remaining untracked before staging.
actionlint is not installed locally, so workflow validation used YAML parsing and structural assertions instead.

tests.name: tests/test_reviewdog_export.py
tests.notes: Covered relative path normalization, function line mapping, non-file finding filtering, severity mapping, rdjsonl writing, and CLI execution.

tests.name: tests
tests.notes: Full pytest regression suite passed with 36 tests.

tests.name: reviewdog exporter smoke validation
tests.notes: Validated 93 generated diagnostics against real repository files: 5 WARNING and 88 INFO.

tests.name: GitHub Actions hosted execution
tests.notes: Hosted GitHub Actions execution requires pushing the workflow to GitHub.

decisions: Kept Repo Manager as a deterministic signal producer and used reviewdog as the publishing adapter.
Added PR-Agent as a separately configured optional reviewer instead of reusing repo_manager_core.health.call_agent.
Pinned third-party GitHub Actions to immutable commit SHAs.
Kept T005 independent from the T001 through T004 benchmark sequence so reviewer tooling can be completed early.
Skipped publishing and PR-Agent execution for fork pull requests rather than using pull_request_target to run untrusted code with write-capable credentials.
risks: PR-Agent remains disabled until the repository variable ENABLE_PR_AGENT is set to true and the OPENAI_KEY secret is configured.
Hosted GitHub Actions execution was not available locally; the workflow was validated through YAML parsing, structural assertions, tests, and a real exporter smoke run.
Fork pull requests run tests and signal generation but do not publish reviewdog comments or run PR-Agent because pull_request tokens are intentionally read-only.

The current repo health scan generated 93 file-backed diagnostics. Reviewdog publishes warning-or-higher findings, but repo-local smell rules should still be calibrated over time through explicit user feedback.

The final local scorer result is 98/100 because five legitimate T005 implementation files remain untracked before staging. They are all inside T005 allowed_paths; no cache, scratch, scope, correctness, handoff, or board-consistency issue remains.
next_recommended_step: Push the branch to exercise the hosted workflow, then set ENABLE_PR_AGENT=true and configure OPENAI_KEY when independent AI PR review is desired.
